title: Entity Component System (ECS)
description: Understand Entity-Component-System (ECS) vs Object-Oriented hierarchies, composition, and cache-friendly loops.
learningObjectives: Differentiate ECS architectures from traditional OOP inheritance
Analyze how flat component arrays maximize CPU L1/L2 cache line hits

sections.title: Data-Oriented Design and Cache Locality
sections.content: OOP inheritance splits class properties and methods. In ECS: Entities are raw IDs, Components are pure data structs, and Systems are pure functions operating on collections. Components are stored in flat, contiguous arrays (one array per component type). Since systems iterate through sequentially packed component lists, the CPU cache operates at peak efficiency, executing calculations (like movements) thousands of times faster than visiting scattered OOP heap nodes.

sections.title: Contiguous Position Updates in ECS
